title: AP 物理C电磁 2025年样题2
description: AP AP 物理C电磁 2025年样题2

scoring.note: Scoring unavailable until answer keys are imported.

questions.prompt:
A grounded spherical conductor is on an insulating stand. A positively charged rod is brought close to the sphere but does not touch the sphere, as shown. The rod is moved far away and then the grounding wire is removed.

Which of the following describes the resulting charge on the sphere?

options.content: Positive
Negative
No net charge and no charge polarization
No net charge, but it is polarized with more negative charges on the left side of the sphere

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
Ammeters A1 and A2 are placed in a circuit that consists of a battery and different resistors R 1 , R2, and R3, as shown. Ammeter A1 measures a current of 500mA and Ammeter A2 measures a current of 300mA.

What is the current in Resistor R 3 ?

options.content: 200mA
250mA
300mA
800mA

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
The figure shows equipotential lines in a region containing three charged objects.

Which of the following correctly indicates the direction of the electric field at Point P and provides a valid justification?

Direction Justification
A The electric field is directed perpendicular to the equipotential lines and toward decreasing electric potential.
B The electric field is in the same direction as the electric force exerted on a negatively charged object.
C The electric field is directed perpendicular to the equipotential lines and toward increasing electric potential.
D The electric field is in the same direction as the electric force exerted on a positively charged object.

options.content: A
B
C
D

questions.prompt: A student constructs an LC circuit using a charged capacitor with capacitance 3.0μF and an inductor with unknown inductance. If the frequency of oscillation of the circuit is 250Hz, what is the inductance of the inductor?

options.content: 0.14H
0.19H
0.82H
210H

questions.prompt:
A current-carrying conducting plate is part of a larger circuit (not shown). The plate is in the plane of the figure, as shown. The conventional current I in the plate is directed to the right, as shown. The plate is in a region of magnetic field directed out of the plane of the figure.

Which of the following correctly compares the electric potential V 1 at Point 1 on the plate to the electric potential V2 at Point 2 on the plate?

options.content: V1<V2
(V1=V2)=0
(V1=V2)>0
V1>V2

questions.prompt:
Two uniformly charged nonconducting spheres, each with diameter D , are placed such that their surfaces are separated by a distance D, as shown. The magnitudes of the charges of the spheres are Q and 2Q, as indicated in the figure.

What is the magnitude of the electric force exerted by one sphere on the other?

options.content: kQ29D2
kQ22D2
kQ2D2
2Q2D2

questions.prompt:
Two spheres each with charge +Q are held fixed on the x-axis at positions x = − L and x=+L, as shown. A positively charged particle with charge +q is placed on the y-axis and released from rest at a position slightly above the origin.

Which graph best represents the particle’s speed v as a function of time t as the particle moves along the y -axis?

questions.prompt:
Sphere 1 of radius R has a spherically symmetric charge density that is described by ρ ( r ) = C r 2 , where C is a constant and r is the radial distance from the center of the sphere. The total charge of Sphere 1 is Q1. Sphere 2 has radius 2R and also has the same spherically symmetric charge density described by ρ(r)=Cr2.

What is the total charge of Sphere 2 ?

options.content: 4Q1
8Q1
16Q1
32Q1

questions.prompt: The plates of an isolated, charged parallel-plate capacitor are moved apart. Which of the following correctly indicates how the capacitance of the capacitor changes and provides a valid justification?

options.content: Decreases, because the amount of charge on the plates of the capacitor decreases.
Decreases, because the potential difference across the capacitor plates increases.
Does not change, because the area of the capacitor plates does not change.
Does not change, because the potential difference increases by the same factor as the distance between the plates increases.

questions.prompt:
A circuit contains three identical light bulbs, X, Y , and Z , and a switch connected to a battery of emf Ɛ, as shown. The switch is initially open and bulbs X and Y have equal brightness, while Bulb Z is not lit.

What happens to the brightness of bulbs X and Y when the switch is closed?

Bulb X Bulb Y
A Remains the same Becomes dimmer
B Remains the same Remains the same
C Becomes brighter Becomes dimmer
D Becomes brighter Remains the same

options.content: A
B
C
D

questions.prompt: The electric field immediately outside of a charged conducting sphere of radius 0.25m is 400N/C. What is the charge density on the surface of the sphere?

options.content: 2.8×10−9Cm2
3.5×10−9Cm2
4.2×10−8Cm2
510Cm2

questions.prompt:
A student is examining two nails, labeled X and Y. When Nail X is connected to a magnet, it can pick up iron filings, but drops roughly half of them when the magnet is removed. When Nail Y is connected to a magnet, it also picks up iron filings, but it loses all of them when the magnet is removed.

Which of the following provides a correct description of the types of materials in these nails and provides valid reasoning?

Nail X Nail Y Reason
A Ferromagnetic Ferromagnetic Both materials become magnetized in the presence of a magnet.
B Ferromagnetic Paramagnetic A ferromagnetic material retains some of the strength of the magnetic field after the magnet is removed. A paramagnetic material loses all its magnetic field strength when the magnet is removed.
C Paramagnetic Ferromagnetic A paramagnetic material retains some of the strength of the magnetic field after the magnet is removed. A ferromagnetic material loses all its magnetic field strength when the magnet is removed.
D Paramagnetic Paramagnetic Both materials become magnetized and drop some of the iron filings when the magnet is removed.

options.content: A
B
C
D

questions.prompt:
Two conducting spheres, A and B, are attached to nonconducting stands. Sphere A has a radius of 0.3m and Sphere 2 has a radius of 0.1m. Both spheres initially have net charges of +10nC. The spheres are brought into contact with one another, and then separated and moved far away from each other.

Which of the following correctly indicates the final charges on the spheres after they are separated and moved far away from each other?

Sphere A Sphere B
A +5nC +15nC
B +10nC +10nC
C +15nC +5nC
D +20nC 0

options.content: A
B
C
D

Which of the following best represents the direction, if any, of the net magnetic field at Point P?

options.content: No direction, because the magnetic field at Point P is zero

questions.prompt:
A parallel-plate capacitor is connected to a battery and has charge +Q on its top plate. The energy stored in the capacitor is U C . While the capacitor remains connected to the battery, the separation between the capacitor plates is doubled.

Which of the following indicates the charge on the top plate and the energy stored in the capacitor after the plate separation is doubled?

Charge on Top Plate Energy Stored in Capacitor
A +2Q 2 U C
B +2Q UC2
C +Q2 2UC
D +Q2 UC2

options.content: A
B
C
D

What is the magnitude of the potential difference across the inductor at time t = 1.0 s ?

0.20V

questions.prompt:
A rectangular conducting loop is located above a long, straight wire carrying a current I to the right, as shown. The wire and loop are both in the plane of the page.

Which of the following actions would cause a clockwise current to be induced in the loop?

options.content: Moving the loop to the right
Moving the loop out of the page while keeping the same orientation
Moving the loop away from the wire
Moving the loop toward the wire

questions.prompt:
A rectangular loop lies in the xy-plane with height b along the y-axis and length a along the x-axis, as shown. The lower left corner of the loop is at the origin. There is an electric field in the entire region that is directed out of the page and with magnitude given by E = C x 2 , where C is a constant with appropriate units.

Which of the following changes would produce the largest change in the electric flux through the loop?

options.content: Doubling the height b while keeping the lower left corner at the origin
Doubling the length a while keeping the lower left corner at the origin
Doubling the constant C while keeping the lower left corner at the origin
Shifting the loop a distance a in the +x-direction

questions.prompt:
A circuit consists of a metal rod with resistance R connected to a battery by wires of negligible resistance. Part of the rod passes through a small region with a magnetic field directed out of the page, as shown. The magnitude of the magnetic force exerted on the current-carrying rod is FB. The circuit is then changed by replacing the rod with a rod that is twice as long, while still made from the same material and with the same cross-sectional area. No changes are made to the magnetic field or the size of the region.

What is the net magnetic force exerted on the new rod?

options.content: FB2
FB
2FB
4FB

Which graph correctly represents the magnetic flux Φ through the loop as a function of time?

questions.prompt:
At the instant shown, a conducting bar is sliding to the right along frictionless, conducting rails while maintaining contact with the rails. The rails are connected to a resistor, as shown. The rails and sliding bar are in a magnetic field that is directed into the page.

Which of the following correctly describes the acceleration of the bar and provides a valid justification?

options.content: The acceleration is zero because the resistor is not in the magnetic field.
The acceleration is zero because the magnetic force creates a charge separation across the ends of the bar but does not oppose the motion of the bar.
The acceleration is nonzero because there will be a current in the bar due to the induced emf and the magnetic field will exert a force on the current-carrying bar.
The acceleration is nonzero because the electric field created by the charge separation in the bar will exert a force on the bar.

What is the final net charge on Sphere X ?

+6×10−7C
